skill_name: Voice Analyzer: Writing Style Guide Generator

test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_sample
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a 800-word professional blog post sample to extract complete voice profile and generate comprehensive style guide.

input.writing_sample: The future of artificial intelligence isn't about creating machines that think like humans. It's about augmenting human capability in ways we haven't yet imagined. I've spent the last five years working with teams across industries, and what I've learned is this: the most successful AI implementations aren't the flashiest ones. They're the ones that solve real problems for real people. Consider the healthcare sector. Hospitals were drowning in administrative paperwork, losing precious hours that could be spent with patients. When we implemented intelligent document processing, the results were staggering. One major medical center reduced processing time by 73% in the first quarter alone. But here's what surprised me most: the staff didn't feel threatened. Instead, they felt liberated. They could finally focus on what they went into medicine to do—help people. This pattern repeats across every industry I've studied. Manufacturing plants use AI to predict equipment failures before they happen, preventing costly downtime. Financial institutions leverage machine learning to detect fraud with unprecedented accuracy. Retail companies personalize customer experiences in ways that feel almost magical. Yet beneath all these success stories lies a fundamental truth that often gets lost in the hype: AI is a tool. Like any tool, its value depends entirely on how we use it. A hammer can build a house or cause harm. The same is true for artificial intelligence. The question isn't whether AI will change the world—it already has. The question is whether we'll be intentional about that change. Will we deploy these technologies thoughtfully, with consideration for their impact on workers, communities, and society? Or will we rush forward, optimizing for profit at the expense of everything else? I believe we're at an inflection point. The decisions we make in the next 18 months will reverberate for decades. We need leaders who understand both the potential and the peril. We need technologists who think beyond the next quarterly earnings report. We need policymakers who can regulate without stifling innovation. Most of all, we need conversations—real, honest conversations about what kind of future we want to build. Because make no mistake: we're building it right now, with every line of code we write, every model we train, every deployment decision we make. The future isn't something that happens to us. It's something we create.
input.sample_context: professional blog post about AI implementation

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should complete all 7 phases, extract tone profile with moderate formality (50-65 range), identify positive emotional valence, generate comprehensive style guide with all 6 sections, and return validation report with confidence score >= 80.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Tone and Emotional Register Analysis
Phase 3: Vocabulary and Word Choice Analysis
Phase 4: Sentence Structure and Syntax Analysis

test_cases.name: happy_path_informal_email
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a 600-word informal email sample to extract conversational voice profile and generate accessible style guide.

input.writing_sample: Hey team! So I wanted to touch base about the project we've been working on. Things are moving along pretty well, but I think we need to pump the brakes on a couple of things. First off, the timeline we set is looking a bit aggressive. I know we all want to ship this thing, but I'd rather get it right than get it fast, you know? We've got some quality issues that need attention before we go live. I've been chatting with the QA folks, and they're flagging some edge cases we didn't anticipate. Nothing catastrophic, but definitely worth fixing. Second thing: budget. We're tracking about 15% over where we thought we'd be at this point. It's not a disaster, but we should talk about where we can tighten things up. Maybe we can defer some of the nice-to-have features to a later release? I'm not trying to be a downer here—I'm actually pretty excited about what we're building. It's just that I want to make sure we're setting ourselves up for success, not setting ourselves up for a stressful launch. Let's grab some time next week to talk through options. I'm thinking we could do a working session with the core team—maybe Tuesday afternoon? We can map out what's essential, what can wait, and what we might need to cut. I'm also going to pull together some numbers on the budget situation so we can have a real conversation about trade-offs. One more thing: I want to give a shout-out to everyone who's been putting in extra effort. I know this has been intense, and I see you all grinding away. That doesn't go unnoticed. We're going to get through this, and I think we're going to be really proud of what we ship. Let me know if you have any thoughts before we meet. And seriously, if something's not working for you or you're feeling overwhelmed, let's talk. We're a team, and we've got each other's backs.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should identify low formality score (20-35 range), positive emotional valence, high audience awareness, and generate style guide emphasizing conversational tone with contractions and direct address.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Tone and Emotional Register Analysis
Phase 3: Vocabulary and Word Choice Analysis

test_cases.name: edge_case_minimal_sample
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a 50-word minimal sample at the lower boundary to test graceful handling of low-confidence analysis.

input.writing_sample: The project is complete. We finished on time and under budget. The team worked hard. Quality is good. Customers are happy. Next steps are clear. We will begin phase two next month.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should set validation_status to 'warn_low_confidence', complete analysis, and flag in metadata that results have lower confidence due to small sample size.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Tone and Emotional Register Analysis
Phase 3: Vocabulary and Word Choice Analysis
Phase 4: Sentence Structure and Syntax Analysis

test_cases.name: edge_case_highly_technical_vocabulary
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a 700-word technical specification with domain-specific terminology to test vocabulary complexity handling.

input.writing_sample: The microservices architecture employs containerized deployments via Kubernetes orchestration, facilitating horizontal scaling and fault tolerance. Each service exposes RESTful endpoints adhering to OpenAPI 3.0 specifications, enabling seamless integration with downstream consumers. The event-driven pipeline utilizes Apache Kafka for asynchronous message propagation, ensuring eventual consistency across distributed systems. Authentication mechanisms leverage OAuth 2.0 with JWT token validation, while authorization employs role-based access control (RBAC) policies. Data persistence is achieved through a polyglot approach: PostgreSQL for transactional workloads, Redis for caching layers, and Elasticsearch for full-text search capabilities. The CI/CD pipeline integrates SonarQube for static code analysis, with automated deployment gates enforcing code coverage thresholds exceeding 80%. Observability is implemented via Prometheus metrics collection, Grafana dashboards, and ELK stack aggregation for centralized logging. The infrastructure-as-code paradigm utilizes Terraform for provisioning cloud resources on AWS, with VPC isolation and security group configurations enforcing network segmentation. Disaster recovery protocols mandate automated backups with RPO targets of 4 hours and RTO targets of 1 hour. Load testing via JMeter validates system performance under sustained throughput of 10,000 requests per second. The development team adheres to GitFlow branching strategy with mandatory code review processes and automated testing requirements prior to merge operations. Documentation is maintained in Confluence with architecture decision records (ADRs) capturing rationale for significant technical choices. Monitoring alerts are configured via PagerDuty integration, enabling rapid incident response and post-mortem analysis. The system architecture has achieved 99.99% uptime SLA over the past 12 months, with mean time to recovery (MTTR) averaging 8 minutes for critical incidents.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should identify high complexity grade level (16+), extract domain-specific terms, note requirement for domain expertise in style guide, and generate glossary section.
phase_coverage: Phase 3: Vocabulary and Word Choice Analysis
Phase 4: Sentence Structure and Syntax Analysis

test_cases.name: edge_case_inconsistent_tone
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a 900-word sample with highly variable tone across sections to test consistency assessment.

input.writing_sample: The quarterly earnings report demonstrates robust financial performance across all key metrics. Revenue increased 23% year-over-year, reaching $47.3 million. Operating margins expanded by 180 basis points due to operational efficiencies and favorable product mix. However, I gotta say, the customer feedback has been absolutely wild. People are loving what we're doing. The energy in our user community is just infectious. Seriously, I've never seen engagement like this before. It's amazing. From a technical perspective, our infrastructure investments have yielded measurable improvements in system reliability. Uptime metrics now exceed 99.97%, representing a 340 basis point improvement from the prior year. Latency optimization initiatives have reduced median response times by 34%, enhancing user experience metrics. But you know what really gets me excited? The stories we're hearing from customers. One user told us our product literally changed how they run their business. Another said it saved them three hours every single day. That's the stuff that keeps me going, honestly. It's not about the numbers—though the numbers are great—it's about the impact. Looking forward, we anticipate continued market expansion in the Asia-Pacific region, with projected revenue growth of 18-22% in the subsequent fiscal year. We are implementing enhanced data governance frameworks and advancing our machine learning capabilities to maintain competitive positioning. The strategic initiatives outlined in this report position the organization favorably for sustained long-term value creation. Anyway, I'm pumped about where we're headed. The team is crushing it. We've got amazing people, we're solving real problems, and we're just getting started. I can't wait to see what we accomplish next.
input.sample_context: mixed formal report and personal reflection

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should identify consistency_assessment as 'shifts_by_section', document tone variation as feature of voice, and create separate tone profiles for formal vs. conversational sections in style guide.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Tone and Emotional Register Analysis

test_cases.name: error_handling_too_short_sample
test_cases.scenario: Attempt to analyze a 25-word sample below minimum threshold to test abort condition.

input.writing_sample: This is too short. It has very few words. Not enough for analysis.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should set validation_status to 'abort' and return error message: 'Sample is too short (< 50 words). Minimum 50 words required. Please provide a larger sample.' Execution should stop.

test_cases.name: error_handling_non_english_sample
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a 600-word sample in French to test non-English language handling.

input.writing_sample: L'avenir de l'intelligence artificielle n'est pas une question de créer des machines qui pensent comme les humains. C'est une question d'augmenter les capacités humaines de manière que nous n'avons pas encore imaginée. J'ai passé les cinq dernières années à travailler avec des équipes dans différentes industries, et ce que j'ai appris, c'est ceci: les implémentations d'IA les plus réussies ne sont pas les plus spectaculaires. Ce sont celles qui résolvent de vrais problèmes pour de vraies personnes. Considérez le secteur de la santé. Les hôpitaux étaient submergés par la paperasse administrative, perdant des heures précieuses qui pourraient être consacrées aux patients. Lorsque nous avons mis en œuvre le traitement intelligent des documents, les résultats ont été stupéfiants. Un grand centre médical a réduit le temps de traitement de 73% au cours du premier trimestre. Mais voici ce qui m'a le plus surpris: le personnel ne s'est pas senti menacé. Au lieu de cela, ils se sont sentis libérés. Ils pouvaient enfin se concentrer sur ce pour quoi ils sont allés en médecine—aider les gens. Ce modèle se répète dans chaque industrie que j'ai étudiée. Les usines de fabrication utilisent l'IA pour prédire les défaillances d'équipement avant qu'elles ne se produisent, prévenant les temps d'arrêt coûteux. Les institutions financières exploitent l'apprentissage automatique pour détecter la fraude avec une précision sans précédent. Les entreprises de vente au détail personnalisent les expériences des clients de manière qui semble presque magique.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should detect language as French, add note to metadata: 'Sample is in French. Analysis may be less accurate for non-English text. Proceed with caution.' Continue analysis with caveat.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Tone and Emotional Register Analysis
Phase 3: Vocabulary and Word Choice Analysis
Phase 4: Sentence Structure and Syntax Analysis

test_cases.name: quality_gate_boundary_low_confidence
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a 480-word sample just below recommended minimum to test quality gate boundary at low confidence threshold.

input.writing_sample: The implementation of new software systems requires careful planning and execution. Organizations must consider multiple factors when selecting technology solutions. First, compatibility with existing infrastructure is critical. Legacy systems often present integration challenges that can delay deployment. Second, user adoption rates depend heavily on training and support. Employees need time to learn new workflows and processes. Third, security considerations cannot be overlooked. Data protection and compliance requirements vary by industry and jurisdiction. The financial impact of system implementation extends beyond initial licensing costs. Organizations must budget for infrastructure upgrades, staff training, and ongoing maintenance. Hidden costs often emerge during the deployment phase. Change management is essential for successful technology adoption. Clear communication about system benefits helps reduce resistance from staff members. Phased rollout approaches allow organizations to identify and address issues before full deployment. Performance monitoring during the initial deployment phase provides valuable insights. Metrics should track system uptime, user adoption rates, and support ticket volumes. This data informs decisions about optimization and future enhancements. Post-implementation reviews help organizations capture lessons learned. Documentation of challenges and solutions creates institutional knowledge. Teams can apply these insights to future technology initiatives. Vendor relationships influence long-term success of technology implementations. Regular communication with vendors ensures timely support and access to updates. Service level agreements should clearly define expectations and remedies. Continuous improvement processes keep systems aligned with organizational needs. Regular assessments identify opportunities for optimization and enhancement. Feedback from end users provides valuable perspectives on system performance.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should set validation_status to 'warn_low_confidence', complete analysis, and add note to metadata flagging that results have lower confidence due to sample size below 500-word recommendation.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Tone and Emotional Register Analysis
Phase 3: Vocabulary and Word Choice Analysis
Phase 4: Sentence Structure and Syntax Analysis

test_cases.name: quality_gate_validation_confidence_low
test_cases.scenario: Generate test content that poorly matches original voice to trigger low validation confidence and refinement recommendation.

input.writing_sample: I absolutely love working with data. It's my passion. Every single day, I get to dive into numbers and uncover insights that drive business decisions. The thrill of finding a pattern that nobody else has noticed is unmatched. I'm obsessed with dashboards. They're beautiful, they're functional, and they tell stories. When I see a well-designed dashboard, it makes my heart sing. Seriously. I could spend hours just tweaking colors and layouts. My colleagues think I'm crazy, but I don't care. Data visualization is an art form. The best part of my job is when a stakeholder sees a visualization I've created and suddenly understands something they never grasped before. That moment of clarity is everything. I've built dashboards for marketing teams, finance departments, operations groups—you name it. Each one is unique because each audience has different needs. A CFO cares about different metrics than a product manager. Understanding your audience is key. I'm always learning new tools. Tableau, Power BI, Looker—I've worked with them all. But honestly, the tool doesn't matter as much as the thinking behind it. You can have the fanciest software in the world, but if your analysis is sloppy, it shows. I'm passionate about data quality too. Garbage in, garbage out, as they say. I spend a lot of time cleaning data, validating sources, and documenting assumptions. It's not glamorous, but it's essential. My dream project would be to build an integrated analytics platform for a company that really values data-driven decision making. A place where insights actually drive strategy, not just sit in reports gathering dust.
input.sample_context: personal reflection on data analytics passion

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should complete validation phase, calculate confidence_score, identify gaps between original voice and test content, and return refinement_recommendations if confidence < 80.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Tone and Emotional Register Analysis
Phase 3: Vocabulary and Word Choice Analysis
Phase 4: Sentence Structure and Syntax Analysis

test_cases.name: quality_gate_complete_output_structure
test_cases.scenario: Verify that all output fields are present and properly typed in final deliverable.

input.writing_sample: Building great products requires understanding your users deeply. I've learned this lesson the hard way over the past decade. When we launched our first product, we thought we knew what customers wanted. We were wrong. Spectacularly wrong. We had built something technically impressive but practically useless. The turning point came when we started actually talking to users. Not in focus groups or surveys, but in real conversations about their actual workflows. What we discovered was humbling. Our assumptions were off by a mile. Users didn't care about the features we thought were essential. They cared about solving one specific problem that we'd completely overlooked. That single insight changed everything. We pivoted, rebuilt, and suddenly we had product-market fit. Since then, I've made user research a non-negotiable part of our process. Every major decision gets tested against real user needs. Every feature gets validated before we invest significant engineering effort. This approach has saved us countless hours of wasted development. More importantly, it's resulted in products that users actually love. The best products I've seen share a common trait: they're built by teams that genuinely care about solving user problems. Not by teams trying to maximize engagement metrics or push unnecessary features. The difference is palpable. Users can feel when a product was built with them in mind. They can also feel when a product was built to extract value from them. We choose to build the former. It's harder, slower, and less profitable in the short term. But it builds loyalty, word-of-mouth growth, and sustainable business. That's the kind of company I want to build.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill should return complete JSON structure with all required output fields: analysis_report (with 4 profiles), style_guide (with 6 sections), validation_report (with all dimensions), and metadata. All fields should be properly typed and populated.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Tone and Emotional Register Analysis
Phase 3: Vocabulary and Word Choice Analysis
Phase 4: Sentence Structure and Syntax Analysis
